page: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and is supported by 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is a key feature of this instance, providing 30,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 123,862.56 and a PassMark CPU score of 86,632.57, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1,891.9 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1,701.0. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 333.72 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 134.52 GB/sec for write operations. The instance demonstrates high efficiency in database operations, achieving 104.77 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a strong PassMark database operations score of 29,745.24.

Given its massive local NVMe SSD storage capacity and high memory density, the i4i.32xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed local data access without the latency of network-attached storage. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio combined with 30 TB of local NVMe SSD makes it highly suitable for large-scale transactional databases, NoSQL databases, data warehousing, and real-time analytics. It is also well-suited for high-throughput web serving and multi-threaded data processing tasks, though the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized instance designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features a 128-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 64 physical cores running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the i4i.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring 123,862.56 in stress-ng multi-core and 86,632.57 in PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is strong, supporting up to 333.72 GB/sec for large-block reads. With strong database performance, including 104.77 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, this instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput analytical workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 30,000 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, storage-optimized instance featuring massive local NVMe SSD storage and top-tier multi-core processing for data-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz.
1024 GB of DDR4 memory paired with 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Top-tier multi-core performance with strong memory read and write bandwidth.
Optimized for large-scale transactional databases, NoSQL, and data warehousing.
